# Entity workflows and today’s journal (Filed / Updated)

## When this applies

Whenever an agent **creates** a new file under `pages/` or **materially edits** an existing graph page while doing **logseq-entity** work—new or updated entity instances, prerequisite pages **when the active type page or registry requires them**, `Logseq/Entity/<Type>` or registry edits, imports that add `logseq-entity::` / `created-by::` / `alias::`, etc.—the task is **not finished** until today’s journal records those graph moves.

This is independent of whether the user asked for a journal note; it is **repo policy** whenever rule **`logseq-core`** (journal updates) applies.

## How to record

Follow **`[[Logseq/Journal]]`** — the single source for the change-log format (**`[[Filed]]`** for new pages, **`[[Updated]]`** for edited ones; mutual exclusivity, link-only lines, and interactive grouping all live there). Do not restate or reinvent those rules here, and do not start a parallel change log for the same day.

## Agent report

End-of-task summary should **confirm** which journal links were added (or state that no graph pages changed).

## Related

- page: **`[[Logseq/Journal]]`** — the journal format (sections, change log, editorial titles).
- rule: `logseq-core` — always-on reminder to record graph edits, deferring to **`[[Logseq/Journal]]`**.
